title: All The Way Down

jokes.text: Oh! In that case, it stinks.
jokes.setup: Professor proudly shows off his simulation of the entire universe
jokes.punchline: Fry's instant dismissal based purely on learning what it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - his opinion changes instantly based on context, not observation. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Bite my shiny middle pixel!
jokes.setup: Roll call of pixelated characters in the simulation
jokes.punchline: Bender's catchphrase adapted to his low-resolution form
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of iconic catchphrase to the visual gag context. Works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Hooray! I'm an unnamed member of the group!
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses everyone else as 'and the rest'
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's enthusiasm for being relegated to background status
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about TV show conventions while staying true to Zoidberg's pathetic optimism. Well-crafted character moment.

jokes.text: My Simputron uses more electricity than 100 bug zappers. Which reminds me, I had to unplug our bug zappers.
jokes.setup: Impressive power consumption comparison to bug zappers
jokes.punchline: Immediate consequence reveals they're now defenseless against bugs
jokes.explanation: Good setup-payoff structure with practical consequences. Classic Professor prioritization humor.

jokes.text: - Ow! - Got him! The mosquito is still alive. There's a mosquito?
jokes.setup: Professor's explanation about unplugging bug zappers
jokes.punchline: Immediate mosquito attack proving his point, followed by oblivious question
jokes.explanation: Solid physical payoff to the bug zapper setup, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Space Italy? I know, it's an embarrassingly lazy name.
jokes.setup: Delivery destination is announced as 'Space Italy'
jokes.punchline: Professor acknowledges the uninspired naming convention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on lazy sci-fi naming conventions. Self-aware humor at its best.

jokes.text: Sometimes, you just have to go with 'Space Italy' or 'the Robot Planet' or 'Dr. Zoidberg.'
jokes.setup: Professor explaining lazy naming in universe creation
jokes.punchline: Implies Zoidberg's name is as uninspired as 'Space Italy'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that lands on character humor. The rule of three works perfectly here, with Zoidberg as the unexpected third example.

jokes.text: Preposterous.
jokes.setup: Professor suggests Zoidberg's name is lazily made up
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's indignant but ineffective protest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg reaction - formal indignation that somehow makes him seem even more ridiculous.

jokes.text: Up all those steps? No grazie. I'll just chuck it into Trevi Fountain for good luck.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's delivering the package in Space Italy
jokes.punchline: Lazy solution disguised as following local customs
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender laziness with cultural awareness. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Papa pia! He hit-a the Space Pope!
jokes.setup: Bender throws package into fountain
jokes.punchline: Package hits the Space Pope with stereotypical Italian reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of Bender's careless action. The Space Pope adds absurdity.

jokes.text: More like three!
jokes.setup: Italian calls Planet Express 'a real two-bit operation'
jokes.punchline: Fry corrects to 'three-bit' referencing the pixelated graphics
jokes.explanation: Clever computer graphics pun that works on multiple levels. Good integration with the simulation concept.

jokes.text: Jeez, Dave! Get a fig leaf!
jokes.setup: Looking at Michelangelo's David statue
jokes.punchline: Fry's prudish reaction to classical art nudity
jokes.explanation: Standard culture clash humor. Fits Fry's character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Oh, mama, they're goin' at it like two packs of gum!
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela are kissing/being romantic
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to gum packages
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd simile. The specificity of 'two packs of gum' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Like my ass?
jokes.setup: Professor explaining how every atom affects everything when something moves
jokes.punchline: Bender offers his ass as an example
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender interjection. Crude but perfectly timed to interrupt serious exposition.

jokes.text: That's a perfect example, Bender, and some pretty decent twerking.
jokes.setup: Bender asks if his ass is a good example of particle physics
jokes.punchline: Professor acknowledges both the physics and Bender's dance moves
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that acknowledges the visual gag we can't see but must infer from context.

jokes.text: In fact, make it my last $10!
jokes.setup: Fry bets his last $5 on shell game
jokes.punchline: Immediately doubles his bet despite not having the money
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry impulsiveness and poor math skills. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: New evidence was presented and I changed my mind. I'm a scientist, not a... - Idiot? - Politician.
jokes.setup: Professor admits to changing his mind, sets up 'not a...' format
jokes.punchline: Chooses 'politician' over the expected 'idiot'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with political commentary. Amy's guess makes the punchline land better.

jokes.text: Whuzzulated? Like whozer's?
jokes.setup: Leela says Bender's personality is 'simulated'
jokes.punchline: Bender's confused garbling of the word
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Bender's confusion, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Why'd you have to tell me? You just ruined my life! My big fat fake life!
jokes.setup: Learning he's artificial intelligence
jokes.punchline: Bender's dramatic overreaction with self-contradictory complaint
jokes.explanation: Good Bender melodrama with the irony that he's complaining about his fake life while acknowledging it's fake.

jokes.text: If you prick me, do I not bleed?
jokes.setup: Bender questioning his artificial nature with Shakespearean reference
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of a robot quoting Shylock
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that's absurd coming from a robot. The Shakespeare adds gravitas to robot angst.

jokes.text: Okay, that was a bad example! Ow! I'm bleeding!
jokes.setup: Bender's rhetorical question about bleeding
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves himself wrong by actually bleeding
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to the Shakespeare setup. The immediate contradiction works well.

jokes.text: I don't think so! I think I might not be able to think!
jokes.setup: Asked if he can think (Descartes reference)
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical statement about thinking about not being able to think
jokes.explanation: Clever philosophical paradox delivered in Bender's confused style. Good wordplay on thinking about thinking.

jokes.text: Bender, how were you leaping through the air that entire time? I wasn't! My first leap missed by a mile, so I leapt again. But I never stopped screaming for a second!
jokes.setup: Question about Bender's apparent continuous leaping
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation that he kept leaping and screaming
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual that we have to imagine. The continuous screaming detail elevates it.

jokes.text: Bender, how were you leaping through the air that entire time?
jokes.setup: Bender was trying to stop the Professor from unplugging simulation
jokes.punchline: Implies Bender was airborne for an impossibly long time
jokes.explanation: Good acknowledgment of visual absurdity. Lampshades the impossible physics of cartoon action.

jokes.text: I, Philip J. Fry, hereby pledge to watch every TV show ever made.
jokes.setup: Fry deciding on a life goal while TV plays
jokes.punchline: Impossibly ambitious yet perfectly Fry-like life goal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry goal - ambitious in scope but ultimately lazy. The formal pledge language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Except clams! To hell with clams!
jokes.setup: Bender defending simulated beings' right to respect like any living creature
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary exception for clams with vehement hatred
jokes.explanation: Brilliant random hatred that undermines Bender's moral stance. The specificity and vehemence make it memorable.

jokes.text: She was my aunt!
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses '10101' as just ones and zeros
jokes.punchline: Amy reveals emotional attachment to binary number
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd emotional beat. Two words that completely recontextualize the binary discussion.

jokes.text: Unlike clams!
jokes.setup: Leela making philosophical point about consciousness
jokes.punchline: Adopts Bender's arbitrary clam hatred
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the clam hatred spreading. Quick and economical.

jokes.text: Why are we in a robot strip club? Because we had to do this in a place that's meaningful to me!
jokes.setup: Question about inappropriate location for ceremony
jokes.punchline: Bender's sincere explanation of strip club's personal significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - treating a strip club as sacred space. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: I barely make any effort to keep myself alive. / Oof!
jokes.setup: Professor asked to care for simulated beings
jokes.punchline: Admits he barely cares for himself, apparently gets hit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment about Professor's self-neglect. The 'Oof!' suggests immediate consequences.

jokes.text: We know! Stop telling us! I didn't even have to go.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions doing his best thinking on the toilet
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just sits there for no reason
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Professor's odd habits. The TMI aspect works.

jokes.text: According to these old maps, which I use in lieu of toilet paper...
jokes.setup: Professor showing old sewer maps
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of using them as toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that fits the Professor's hygiene standards. Serviceable but not inspired.

jokes.text: Free clean power! / Clean? / Oh my, no.
jokes.setup: Professor promises clean power from sewage
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits it's not clean at all
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal. Professor's casual correction of his own sales pitch works well.

jokes.text: Contact in four! Three! Number two!
jokes.setup: Standard countdown sequence in sewer context
jokes.punchline: Says 'number two' instead of 'two' in bathroom humor context
jokes.explanation: Basic toilet humor pun. Gets the job done but lacks creativity.

jokes.text: Contact in four! Three! Number two!
jokes.setup: Professor counts down to sewer contact
jokes.punchline: Says 'Number two' instead of 'Two' given the sewage context
jokes.explanation: Obvious toilet humor pun. Predictable but fits the context and Professor's character.

jokes.text: Let's rarely speak of that again.
jokes.setup: Presumably disgusting sewer breakthrough
jokes.punchline: Mild understatement about never discussing it
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to gross situation. 'Rarely' instead of 'never' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Let's rarely speak of that again.
jokes.setup: Apparent sewage disaster just occurred
jokes.punchline: Professor's mild understatement about traumatic event
jokes.explanation: 'Rarely' instead of 'never' is a nice touch. Good deadpan response to disaster.

jokes.text: Aw, they're so cute! Now I care about them!
jokes.setup: Simulated beings are now higher resolution
jokes.punchline: Amy's caring is directly tied to visual appeal
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on how visual quality affects empathy. Very Amy-like shallow response.

jokes.text: What is it, Professor? / No, him. / What is it, Professor?
jokes.setup: Two Professors make breakthrough announcements
jokes.punchline: Confusion about which Professor is being addressed
jokes.explanation: Good confusion from parallel simulations. Meta-humor about identical situations.

jokes.text: A simulation... Within a simulation!? Where does it all end? - Hopefully there.
jokes.setup: Discovery of nested simulation creates existential question
jokes.punchline: Professor's pragmatic hope to stop the recursion
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of existential dread with practical concern. Nice contrast between cosmic and mundane.

jokes.text: You sound like a stoned freshman!
jokes.setup: Leela asking philosophical questions about simulation theory
jokes.punchline: Professor dismisses deep thoughts as drug-addled student behavior
jokes.explanation: Good academic put-down that deflates philosophical discussion. Fits Professor's academic elitism.

jokes.text: Nailed it!
jokes.setup: Professor's dismissive comment about stoned freshman theories
jokes.punchline: Fry's proud admission that he is exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - completely missing the insult and taking pride in it. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, nothing. I just had a loogie in my throat. Possibly a simulated loogie.
jokes.setup: Appears to have important realization about simulation theory
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation with meta-awareness of simulation context
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment. The 'simulated loogie' shows awareness of the themes.

jokes.text: Oh, my glorsh!
jokes.setup: Amy having a realization
jokes.punchline: Her characteristic made-up exclamation
jokes.explanation: Standard Amy character trait. Not particularly funny but consistent with her speech patterns.

jokes.text: Actually, I'm not. Is this about a new soda?
jokes.setup: Complex scientific discussion about computation
jokes.punchline: Fry admits he's lost and guesses it's about soda
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry confusion reducing complex science to consumer products. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Like playing a new video game on a crummy underpowered game system! That my cheapskate grandma got me.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining computational overload through gaming metaphor
jokes.punchline: Personal detail about grandmother's cheap gifts
jokes.explanation: Good personal detail that adds to the gaming metaphor. Feels authentic to Fry's background.

jokes.text: That my cheapskate grandma got me.
jokes.setup: Discussion of underpowered computers
jokes.punchline: Fry relates it to his personal gaming experience with cheap grandma
jokes.explanation: Good personal detail that grounds cosmic discussion in relatable experience. Classic Fry.

jokes.text: Guess again, loser.
jokes.setup: Bender talking about resting secure in knowing he's real
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by calling himself a loser
jokes.explanation: Good Bender self-deprecation that undercuts his previous statement. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Honestly. I wanna know if I'm not living in a simulation, but not if I am. Does that make sense? Surprisingly, yes.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining his contradictory position on learning the truth
jokes.punchline: Leela's admission that his illogical stance makes sense
jokes.explanation: Good philosophical paradox that captures human nature perfectly. Leela's response validates the absurdity.

jokes.text: Oh, I never should have outsourced it to that Indian middle schooler.
jokes.setup: Professor worried about his software's limitations
jokes.punchline: Reveals his universe simulation was coded by a child
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that explains software problems. Absurd but believable for the Professor.

jokes.text: like a big flaming ball of Microsoft Word
jokes.setup: Describing how a magnetar will glitch out
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to notoriously buggy software
jokes.explanation: Good tech humor that everyone can relate to. Microsoft Word as universal symbol of software frustration.

jokes.text: Plus, I like delivering bad news.
jokes.setup: Bender wants to tell simulated beings they're not real
jokes.punchline: Admits he enjoys causing distress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - mixing noble purpose with petty cruelty. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Since you're basically just software in a greasy wad of aluminum foil... Aw...
jokes.setup: Professor describing Bender's technical nature
jokes.punchline: Bender's hurt reaction to unflattering but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's vanity despite being technically accurate description.

jokes.text: It's Benders all the way down.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happens to displaced Benders in nested simulations
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'turtles all the way down' philosophical concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical reference that perfectly captures the infinite recursion concept. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: I don't wanna live in a world where I'm less real than everyone else. Those are my people in there, in Baloney Town.
jokes.setup: Bender's motivation for entering simulation
jokes.punchline: Calls the simulated world 'Baloney Town'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with amusing nickname. 'Baloney Town' perfectly captures Bender's casual dismissiveness while showing affection.

jokes.text: Those are my people in there, in Baloney Town.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining why he wants to help the simulated beings
jokes.punchline: Calls the simulation 'Baloney Town' while being sincere
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Bender shows genuine care despite using dismissive nickname. The contradiction works.

jokes.text: Those are my people in there, in Baloney Town.
jokes.setup: Bender explains why he wants to join the simulation
jokes.punchline: Calls the simulated world 'Baloney Town' (fake/phony)
jokes.explanation: Good slang term for fake world. Shows Bender's acceptance of artificiality while claiming kinship.

jokes.text: How will I go on without you? I'll get a broom.
jokes.setup: Dramatic question about surviving Bender's departure
jokes.punchline: Practical solution focused on cleaning up Bender's mess
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of dramatic moment with practical concern. Perfectly timed and economical.

jokes.text: Probably a bad clam.
jokes.setup: Bender feeling sick during reboot process
jokes.punchline: Blames illness on clams despite earlier hatred of them
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Bender's inconsistency - now blaming clams for his problems rather than just hating them.

jokes.text: I think it makes no difference at all. Either way, the laws of the universe are way beyond our control. So, what can we do? We just make the best of it.
jokes.setup: Existential crisis about living in a simulation
jokes.punchline: Pragmatic acceptance that renders the question meaningless
jokes.explanation: Thoughtful philosophical resolution that's both funny and meaningful. Perfect Leela wisdom cutting through the anxiety.

jokes.text: I think it makes no difference at all. Either way, the laws of the universe are way beyond our control. So, what can we do? We just make the best of it.
jokes.setup: Group debates significance of simulation theory
jokes.punchline: Fry delivers surprisingly wise philosophical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Unexpected wisdom from Fry. Character development moment where the fool becomes sage.

jokes.text: I feel, therefore I am. It doesn't matter if I'm real? Not one bit.
jokes.setup: Twist on Descartes' famous quote
jokes.punchline: Bender's version focuses on feeling rather than thinking
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier philosophy with Bender's emotional take. Good character growth moment.

jokes.text: I feel, therefore I am. It doesn't matter if I'm real? Not one bit.
jokes.setup: Fry's wisdom about reality not mattering
jokes.punchline: Bender adapts Descartes to emotions rather than thought
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical evolution. Bender moving from thinking to feeling as proof of existence shows character growth.

jokes.text: That's robots for you.
jokes.setup: Bender's emotional speech about feeling and reality
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual dismissal as typical robot behavior
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bender's philosophical moment. Fry's obliviousness to the depth works well.

jokes.text: Are we talking regular slow or bureaucrat slow? Infinitely slower than that.
jokes.setup: Question about how slow the simulation will run
jokes.punchline: Bureaucrat slowness as baseline for something even slower
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect character voice from Hermes. The bureaucracy reference is perfectly timed.

tmdb_overview: The crew investigates whether the universe is a simulation.
